FOREWORD
The 1954 General Assembly imposed upon the Legislative
Research Commission the duty of furnishing the printer correct
copy of bills and resolutions for inclusion in the Kentucky Acts .
Section 57.160 of the Kentucky Revised Statutes stipulates that
bound copies of Kentucky Acts be delivered and distributed
within sixty days after the end of a session .
In the preparation of this volume of the Kentucky Acts of
the First Extraordinary Session of 1963 the Secretary of State has
furnished access to copy of filed enrolled bills and resolutions .
The staff of the Legislative Research Commission compared the
prepared page proofs with the enrolled bills. Mr. Courtenay J.
Walker , Administrative Assistant to the Director , has had pri-
mary responsibility for the preparation of this volume .
Frankfort , Kentucky
March 1, 1963
WILSON W. WYATT
Chairman
CHARLES WHEELER
Director

ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
OF THE
COMMONWEALTH OF KENTUCKY
PASSED AT THE EXTRAORDINARY SESSION OF THE GENERAL
ASSEMBLY, WHICH WAS BEGUN IN THE CITY OF FRANKFORT ,
KENTUCKY , ON MONDAY , JANUARY THE TWENTY -EIGHTH , 1963 ,
AND ENDED THURSDAY , FEBRUARY THE TWENTY - EIGHTH , 1963 .
CHAPTER 1
(H. R. 5)
A JOINT RESOLUTION making an appropriation to the Legislative
Research Commission for expenses incurred in connection with the
First Extraordinary Session of 1963 of the General Assembly , and
declaring an emergency .
WHEREAS , additional personnel are necessary to properly expedite the
business of the Senate and the House of Representativs ; and
WHEREAS , the present appropriation of the Legislative Research Com-
mission is insufficient to provide adequate service to the General
Assembly ;
NOW , THEREFORE ,
Be it resolved by the General Assembly of the Commonwealth
of Kentucky :
Section 1. That there be appropriated out of the General
Expenditure Fund , to the Legislative Sessions Fund , for the pay-
ment of salaries and other expenses of the First Extraordinary
Session of 1963 of the General Assembly , the sum of nine thous-
and dollars per calendar day during the First Extraordinary Ses-
sion of 1963 .
Section 2. That there be appropriated out of the General
Expenditure Fund, to the Legislative Research Commission , for
the purpose of providing additional services to the General As-
sembly during the First Extraordinary Session of 1963 of the
General Assembly , the sum of one thousand seven hundred and
fifty dollars per calendar day during the First Extraordinary
Session of 1963 .
Section 3. The appropriations made in Sections 1 and 2 of
this resolution shall be available for expenditure during the
1962-1963 fiscal year .

2 ACTS , FIRST EXTRAORDINARY SESSION
Section 4. Whereas , the General Assembly is now in ses-
sion and it is necessary that funds be available immediately , an
emergency is declared to exist and this resolution shall become
effective upon
itspassage and approval by the Governor
, and
shall
beretroactive
toinclude payment
ofcompensation
tosuch
personnel from and including January
28, 1963
.
Approved February
7, 1963

CHAPTER 2
(S. B. 4)
AN ACT relating to state senatorial districts .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the Commonwealth
of Kentucky :
Section 1 : KRS 6.010 is amended to read as follows :
The Commonwealth of Kentucky is divided into senatorial
districts as follows :
(1) First District : Carlisle , Hickman , Fulton , Graves and
Marshall Counties .
(2) Second District: Ballard , McCracken and Livingston
Counties .
(3) Third District: Pike County .
(4) Fourth District: Henderson , Union, Webster and Crit-
tenden Counties .
(5) Fifth District: Muhlenberg , Todd , Logan , Simpson and
Butler Counties .
(6) Sixth District: Lyon , Caldwell, Hopkins and McLean
Counties .
(32 ) Thirty -second District : Breckinridge , Ohio , Grayson ,
Edmonson and Warren Counties .
(8) Eighth District : Daviess and Hancock Counties .
(9) Ninth District: Larue , Hart, Barren , Metcalfe and
Allen Counties .
(10) Tenth District: Meade and Hardin Counties .
(11) Eleventh District : Campbell County .
(12) Twelfth District : That part of the County of Fayette
which is outside of the corporate limits of the City of Lexington .
(13) Thirteenth District: That part of the County of Fay-
ette comprising the City of Lexington .
(14) Fourteenth District: Bullitt , Nelson , Washington ,
Marion and Boyle Counties .
(15) Fifteenth District: McCreary, Pulaski , Lincoln , Casey
and Taylor Counties .
(16) Sixteenth District: Monroe , Cumberland , Clinton , Rus-
sell , Adair , Green and Wayne Counties .

or precinct means that city , ward, district or precinct as
its
boundaries existed on the effective date of this Act
.
Section
3.
Inthe event any Senator now elected should re

CHAPTER 2 7
sign or die prior to the expiration of his term , the vacancy shall
be filled by an election for the unexpired term , as now provided
by law, in the Senatorial District as defined in Chapter 1 , Acts
of the General Assembly , Extraordinary Session Begun on March
10 , 1942 , and Ended on April 8, 1942 .
Section 4. WHEREAS ; Acts of the General Assembly ordin-
arily take effect ninety days after a Legislative Session, and the
closing of the filing for primary candidates running for nomina-
tion for election from Senatorial Districts One , Three , Five , Seven ,
Nine , Eleven , Thirteen , Fifteen , Seventeen , Nineteen , Twenty-
one, Twenty-three , Twenty-five , Twenty -seven, Twenty-nine ,
Thirty- one , Thirty -three , Thirty -five and Thirty -seven for terms
beginning January 1 , 1964 fall within this time period an emer-
gency is found to exist and this Act shall take effect upon
signature by the Governor .
Approved February 28 , 1963

CHAPTER 3
(Н. В. 3)
AN ACT relating to Legislative Districts .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the Commonwealth
of Kentucky :
tricts :

(2 ) Second District. Graves County .

which on October 1, 1942 embraced the following voting pre-

Grahamville No. 1 , Grahamville No. 2, Rossington , and Ragland .

which on October 1 , 1942 embraced the following voting pre-
cincts : Berry , North Side Court House , South Side Court House

Glauber, Budde , Young , South Side Fire Station , Farley, Butler ,
